focus: DaemonClient.index cancellation semantics + protocol cancel-surface audit + remaining create_task survey
noveltyJustification: Three new findings (F-019 protocol-blocking RPC, F-020 missing CancelIndexRequest, F-021 foreground refresh worker-survives-cancel) plus a cluster-split refinement reframing F-017/F-018 as caller-side symptom and elevating F-019/F-020 as prerequisite root-cause packet.
summary: DaemonClient.index is blocking with no abort surface; protocol exposes only StopRequest and RemoveProjectRequest, no CancelIndexRequest with reqId; foreground refresh in server.py shares the same ThreadPool-survives-cancel hazard. F-017/F-018 remediation requires a sibling protocol-level cancel packet to deliver real cancellation.

title: DaemonClient.index is fully blocking with no cancellation surface; only Connection.close() can break the recv loop
Daemon protocol exposes no CancelIndexRequest; only StopRequest (whole daemon) and RemoveProjectRequest with undefined in-flight-index semantics
server.py foreground refresh+refresh_index handlers share the same ThreadPool-survives-cancel hazard as F-017/F-018, masked by asyncio.wait_for timeout response

Cluster split: F-005/F-010/F-017/F-018 are caller-side symptoms; F-019/F-020 are root-cause protocol gap; split into two packets where protocol packet is prerequisite for task-discipline packet to deliver real cancellation

note: Complete tree-wide create_task inventory across .opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/mcp_server/cocoindex_code/: 5 sites total. daemon.py:378 orphaned (F-005/F-010), daemon.py:630 referenced+awaited via progress queue (correct), daemon.py:1244 tracked in handler task_set with add_done_callback (correct), cli.py:1204 orphaned (F-017), server.py:557 orphaned (F-018). Zero create_task sites in retrieval/, indexer/, observability/, core/. Zero asyncio.ensure_future sites anywhere.

Only one threading.Thread site in cocoindex_code/: daemon.py:1277 accept_thread = threading.Thread(target=_accept_loop, daemon=True). Normal accept-loop pattern, daemon=True so it dies with the process, not a leak. Zero custom ThreadPoolExecutor(...) constructions; every run_in_executor uses the default None executor.

Zero atexit and zero signal.signal hooks in .opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/mcp_server/cocoindex_code/. Process-exit cleanup is fully implicit and relies on asyncio.run's loop.shutdown_default_executor() draining the default ThreadPoolExecutor. Any pending client.index RPC blocks shutdown until the daemon-side index completes.

DaemonClient.close() at core/client.py:201-205 closes the underlying multiprocessing.connection.Connection, which would raise EOFError -> RuntimeError('Connection to daemon lost during indexing') inside the blocking recv_bytes loop at line 97. This is the ONLY caller-side abort primitive for an in-flight index. The F-017/F-018 fire-and-forget pattern stores no client reference, so this abort path is unreachable from the bg-index task.

Remediation design for combined F-017..F-021 packet pair: (1) Add CancelIndexRequest(project_root, reqId) tag to Request union. (2) Add reqId field to IndexRequest. (3) Daemon-side: hold in-flight index task per (project_root, reqId), cancel on receive, return IndexResponse(success=False, message='cancelled'). (4) Client-side: DaemonClient.cancel_index(reqId) API. (5) Caller hygiene: F-017/F-018 _bg_index dedup + reference capture + add_done_callback + shutdown hook that calls cancel_index then closes connection with bounded join.

hypothesis: Additional asyncio.create_task sites exist in retrieval/, indexer/, observability/, core/ subtrees of cocoindex_code/
reason: Tree-wide grep for asyncio.create_task|create_task( returned exactly 5 hits: daemon.py:378, daemon.py:630, daemon.py:1244, cli.py:1204, server.py:557. All five accounted for across iter-003 and iter-004. No matches in retrieval/, indexer/, observability/, core/.

hypothesis: asyncio.ensure_future or background helpers exist as an alternative task-creation pattern outside create_task
reason: Tree-wide grep for ensure_future|threading.Thread|Thread(|ThreadPoolExecutor(|concurrent.futures|atexit|signal.signal in cocoindex_code/ returned exactly one threading.Thread hit (daemon.py:1277 accept loop, daemon=True). Zero ensure_future, zero custom ThreadPoolExecutor, zero atexit, zero signal.signal.

hypothesis: DaemonClient exposes a per-RPC abort or timeout surface beyond close() and stop()
reason: Read core/client.py end-to-end (594 lines). The DaemonClient class exposes only handshake/index/search/project_status/daemon_status/remove_project/stop/close. None of the streaming RPC methods accept a timeout or cancellation token. The only abort primitives are connection.close() (raises EOFError in recv) and StopRequest (kills the whole daemon).

hypothesis: core/protocol.py declares an unused CancelIndexRequest or per-RPC reqId field on IndexRequest that the daemon could honor today
reason: Read core/protocol.py end-to-end. IndexRequest carries only project_root (line 17-18), no reqId. Request union (lines 13-55) does not include any Cancel or Abort tag. The only request-level reqId field exists on SearchRequest (line 28), and is used purely for response correlation.
